22.text: When it is about to befall them, You will see the wrongdoers fearful because of what they have earned; but those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be in the gardens of paradise: they will have whatever they wish near their Lord. That is the mighty grace.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: You shall see the harmdoers in fear of what they have earned as it is about to fall on them. But those who believe and do good deeds shall live in the meadows of the Gardens and from their Lord they will have all that they desire that is the great bounty.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: You will see the wrongdoers fearful of what they have earned, and it will [certainly] befall them. And those who have believed and done righteous deeds will be in lush regions of the gardens [in Paradise] having whatever they will in the presence of their Lord. That is what is the great bounty.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: You can see that the unjust are afraid of the consequences of their deeds which will inevitably strike them. However, the righteously striving believers will live in the gardens wherein they will have whatever they want from their Lord. This is certainly the greatest reward.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: You will see the unjust fearing on account of what they have earned, and it must befall them; and those who believe and do good shall be in the meadows of the gardens; they shall have what they please with their Lord: that is the great grace.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: You will see the wrongdoers fearful of the consequences of their deeds, which will be inescapable. Whereas, those who have believed and done righteous deeds, will be in the meadows of the Garden and shall have whatever they desire from their Lord. That will be the supreme favour.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Thou wilt see the Wrong-doers in fear on account of what they have earned, and (the burden of) that must (necessarily) fall on them. But those who believe and work righteous deeds will be in the luxuriant meads of the Gardens: they shall have, before their Lord, all that they wish for. That will indeed be the magnificent Bounty (of Allah).

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Tara alththalimeena mushfiqeena mimma kasaboo wahuwa waqiAAun bihim waallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati fee rawdati aljannati lahum ma yashaoona AAinda rabbihim thalika huwa alfadlu alkabeeru

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: This is the good news that God gives to His creatures who believe and do good. Say: "I ask no recompense of you for it other than obligations of relationship." We shall give more excellence to him who acquires excellence. Surely God forgives and accepts (gratitude).

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: This is what Allah gives the glad tidings of to His bondmen who accept faith and do good deeds; say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) “I do not ask any fee from you upon this, except the love between close ones”; and whoever performs a good deed – We further increase the goodness in it for him; indeed Allah is Oft Forgiving, Most Appreciative.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: That is the good tidings God gives to His servants who believe and do righteous deeds. Say: 'I do not ask of you a wage for this, except love for the kinsfolk; and whosoever gains a good deed, We shall give him increase of good in respect of it. Surely God is All-forgiving, All-thankful.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: That is that whereof Allah giveth the glad tidings Unto His bondmen who believe and work righteous works, Say thou: I ask of you no hire therefor save affection in respect of kinship. And whoso ever earneth a good deed We shall increase Unto him good in respect thereof; verily Allah is Forgiving, Appreciative.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: That is (the Paradise) whereof Allah gives glad tidings to His slaves who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds. Say (O Muhammad SAW): "No reward do I ask of you for this except to be kind to me for my kinship with you." And whoever earns a good righteous deed, We shall give him an increase of good in respect thereof. Verily, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Ready to appreciate (the deeds of those who are obedient to Him).

23.author: Itani
23.text: That is the good news God gives to His servants who believe and do good deeds. Say, “I ask of you no wage for it, except affection among the near of kin.” Whoever does a good deed, We will increase its goodness for him. God is Forgiving and Appreciative.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: That is the Bounty of which Allah gives tidings to His servants who have faith and do good deeds. Tell them, (O Prophet): “I do not ask you for any recompense for my work except love towards kinsfolk.” Whoever does a good deed, We shall increase its merit for him. Surely Allah is Most Forgiving, Most Appreciative.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: That is whereof Allah gives glad tidings to His servants who believe and do righteous good deeds. Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this except to be kind to me for my kinship with you." And whoever earns a good righteous deed, We shall give him an increase of good in respect thereof. Verily, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Ready to appreciate.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: This it is which Allah announceth unto His bondmen who believe and do good works. Say (O Muhammad, unto mankind): I ask of you no fee therefor, save lovingkindness among kinsfolk. And whoso scoreth a good deed We add unto its good for him. Lo! Allah is Forgiving, Responsive.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Such is the good news that Allah gives to His servants who have faith and do righteous deeds! Say, ‘I do not ask you any reward for it except the love of [my] relatives.’ Whoever performs a good deed, We shall enhance its goodness for him. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-appreciative.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: This is the glad tidings that Allah gives to His worshipers, who believe and do good works. Say: 'For this I ask of you no wage except the love of the (Prophet's) relatives. We will add good to whosoever gains a good deed. Allah is the Forgiving and the Thanker'

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: It is that of which Allah gives good tidings to His servants who believe and do righteous deeds. Say, [O Muhammad], "I do not ask you for this message any payment [but] only good will through kinship." And whoever commits a good deed - We will increase for him good therein. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Appreciative.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: This is the glad news which God gives to His servants, the righteously striving believers. (Muhammad), say, "I do not ask you for any payment for my preaching to you except (your) love of (my near) relatives." Whoever achieves virtue will have its merit increased. God is All-forgiving and Appreciating.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: That is of which Allah gives the good news to His servants, (to) those who believe and do good deeds. Say: I do not ask of you any reward for it but love for my near relatives; and whoever earns good, We give him more of good therein; surely Allah is Forgiving, Grateful.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: These are the glad tidings which God gives to His servants who believe and do righteous deeds. Say, "I do not ask of you any reward for it, except [that I am inviting you to God because of] love of kinship." Whoever earns a good deed, We shall increase its good for him; God is most forgiving, most appreciative.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: That is (the Bounty) whereof Allah gives Glad Tidings to His Servants who believe and do righteous deeds. Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this except the love of those near of kin." And if any one earns any good, We shall give him an increase of good in respect thereof: for Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Ready to appreciate (service).

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Thalika allathee yubashshiru Allahu AAibadahu allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati qul la asalukum AAalayhi ajran illa almawaddata fee alqurba waman yaqtarif hasanatan nazid lahu feeha husnan inna Allaha ghafoorun shakoorun

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Do they say he has fabricated a lie about God? He could have sealed your hearts if He pleased; but God blots out the false and vindicates the truth by His dispensations, for He knows the secrets of the hearts.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: What! They dare say that, “He has fabricated a lie against Allah”? And if Allah wills, He can seal your heart by His mercy and protection*; and Allah wipes out falsehood and proves the truth by His Words; indeed He knows what lies within the hearts. (* So that you may not be agonised by what they do).

24.author: Arberry
24.text: Or do they say, 'He has forged against God a lie?' But if God wills, He will set a seal on thy heart; and God blots out falsehood and verifies the truth by His words; He knows the thoughts within the breasts.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Say they: he hath fabricated a lie concerning God! Now, if Allah willed, He would seal thine heart. And Allah abolisheth falsehood and establisheth truth by His words; verily He is the Knower of that which is in the breasts.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Or say they: "He has invented a lie against Allah?" If Allah willed, He could have sealed your heart (so that you forget all that you know of the Quran). And Allah wipes out falsehood, and establishes the truth (Islam) by His Word (this Qur'an). Verily, He knows well what (the secrets) are in the breasts (of mankind).

24.author: Itani
24.text: Or do they say, “He forged a lie about God.” If God so willed, He could have sealed your heart. But God obliterates the false, and confirm the true by His Words. He knows what is in the hearts.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: Do they say: “He has forged a lie against Allah?” If Allah so wanted He could seal up your heart. Allah blots out falsehood and confirms the truth by His Words. He is well aware of all the secrets hidden in the breasts (of people).

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Or say they: "He has invented a lie against Allah" If Allah willed, He could have sealed up your heart. And Allah wipes out falsehood, and establishes the truth by His Word. Verily, He knows well what are in the breasts.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Or say they: He hath invented a lie concerning Allah? If Allah willed, He could have sealed thy heart (against them). And Allah will wipe out the lie and will vindicate the truth by His words. Lo! He is Aware of what is hidden in the breasts (of men).

24.author: Qarai
24.text: Do they say, ‘He has fabricated a lie against Allah’? If so, should Allah wish He would set a seal on your heart, and Allah will efface the falsehood and confirm the truth with His words. Indeed He knows well what is in the breasts.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: Or do they say: 'He has forged a lie about Allah? But if Allah wills He could set a seal upon your heart. Allah wipes out falsehood and verifies the truth by His Words. He knows the innermost of the chests.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: Or do they say, "He has invented about Allah a lie"? But if Allah willed, He could seal over your heart. And Allah eliminates falsehood and establishes the truth by His words. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: Do they say that he, (Muhammad), has invented falsehood against God? Had God wanted, He could have sealed up your heart. God causes falsehood to vanish and, by His words, firmly establishes the truth. He has full knowledge of what the hearts contain.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Or do they say: He has forged a lie against Allah? But if Allah pleased, He would seal your heart; and Allah will blot out the falsehood and confirm the truth with His words; surely He is Cognizant of what is in the breasts.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: Do they say, "He has invented a lie about God"? If God so willed, He could seal your heart. God wipes out falsehood and vindicates the truth by His words. He has full knowledge of what is in men's hearts --

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: What! Do they say, "He has forged a falsehood against Allah"? But if Allah willed, He could seal up thy heart. And Allah blots out Vanity, and proves the Truth by His Words. For He knows well the secrets of all hearts.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Am yaqooloona iftara AAala Allahi kathiban fain yashai Allahu yakhtim AAala qalbika wayamhu Allahu albatila wayuhiqqu alhaqqa bikalimatihi innahu AAaleemun bithati alssudoori

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: It is He who accepts repentance from His creatures and forgives their trespasses, for He knows what you do.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And it is He Who accepts repentance from His bondmen, and pardons sins*, and knows all your deeds. (Repentance for the cardinal sins, while lesser sins are wiped out by good deeds.)

25.author: Arberry
25.text: It is He who accepts repentance from His servants, and pardons evil deeds; He knows the things you do.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And He it is Who accepteth repentances from His bondmen, and pardoneth the evil deeds and knoweth that which ye do.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And He it is Who accepts repentance from His slaves, and forgives sins, and He knows what you do.

25.author: Itani
25.text: It is He who accepts the repentance of His worshipers, and remits the sins, and knows what you do.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: He it is Who accepts repentance from His servants and forgives sins and knows all what you do,

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And He it is Who accepts repentance from His servants, and forgives sins, and He knows what you do.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: And He it is Who accepteth repentance from His bondmen, and pardoneth the evil deeds, and knoweth what ye do,

25.author: Qarai
25.text: It is He who accepts the repentance of His servants, and excuses their misdeeds and knows what you do.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: It is He who accepts the repentance of His worshipers and pardons their evil deeds. He has knowledge of what you do.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And it is He who accepts repentance from his servants and pardons misdeeds, and He knows what you do.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: It is He who accepts the repentance of His servants, forgives their evil deeds and knows all about what you do.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And He it is Who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons the evil deeds and He knows what you do;

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: He accepts repentance from His servants and pardons their sins. He knows everything you do.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: He is the One that accepts repentance from His Servants and forgives sins: and He knows all that ye do.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Wahuwa allathee yaqbalu alttawbata AAan AAibadihi wayaAAfoo AAani alssayyiati wayaAAlamu ma tafAAaloona

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: He answers (the prayers) of those who believe and do good, and gives them more of His bounty. But for the unbelievers there is severe punishment.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: And He accepts the prayers of those who accept faith and do good deeds, and gives them a greater reward by His munificence; and for the disbelievers is a severe punishment.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: And He answers those who believe and do righteous deeds, and He gives them increase of His bounty. And the unbelievers -- for them awaits a terrible chastisement.

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And He answereth those who believe and work righteous works and increaseth Unto them of His grace. And the infidels! theirs shall be a severe torment.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: And He answers (the invocation of) those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, and gives them increase of His Bounty. And as for the disbelievers, theirs will be a severe torment.

26.author: Itani
26.text: And He answers those who believe and do good deeds, and He increases them of His grace. But the disbelievers will suffer a terrible punishment.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: and answers the prayers of those who believe and do good deeds and bestows upon them even more out of His Bounty. As for those who deny (the Truth), a grievous chastisement awaits them.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: And He answers those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and gives them increase of His bounty. And as for the disbelievers, theirs will be a severe torment.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: And accepteth those who do good works, and giveth increase unto them of His bounty. And as for disbelievers, theirs will be an awful doom.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: He answers [the supplications of] those who have faith and do righteous deeds and enhances them out of His grace. But as for the faithless, there is a severe punishment for them.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: He answers those who believe and do good deeds, and He increases them from His bounty. But for the unbelievers there is a terrible punishment.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And He answers [the supplication of] those who have believed and done righteous deeds and increases [for] them from His bounty. But the disbelievers will have a severe punishment.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: He answers the prayers of the righteously striving believers and grants them increasing favors. The unbelievers will suffer a severe punishment.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And He answers those who believe and do good deeds, and gives them more out of His grace; and (as for) the unbelievers, they shall have a severe punishment.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: He responds to those who believe and do good deeds, and gives them more of His bounty; agonizing torment awaits the  deniers of the truth.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: And He listens to those who believe and do deeds of righteousness, and gives them increase of His Bounty: but for the Unbelievers their is a terrible Penalty.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Wayastajeebu allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati wayazeeduhum min fadlihi waalkafiroona lahum AAathabun shadeedun

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: If God were to give in abundance to His creatures they would fill the earth with oppression. So He gives according to measure as He will. He knows (what is good for) His creatures. He is all-aware and all-seeing.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And had Allah increased the sustenance for all His slaves, they would have surely caused turmoil in the land, but He sends it down by a proper assessment as He wills; indeed He is Well Aware of, Seeing His bondmen.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Had God expanded His provision to His servants, they would have been insolent in the earth; but He sends down in measure whatsoever He will; surely He is aware of and sees His servants.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And had Allah expanded the provision for His bondmen they surely would have rebelled in the earth, but He sendeth down by measure as He willeth; verily He is, in respect of His bondmen. Aware and Beholder.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And if Allah were to enlarge the provision for His slaves, they would surely rebel in the earth, but He sends down by measure as He wills. Verily! He is in respect of His slaves, the Well-Aware, the All-Seer (of things that benefit them).

27.author: Itani
27.text: If God were to increase the provision to His servants, they would transgress on earth; but He sends down in precise measure whatever He wills. Surely, regarding His servants, He is Expert and Observant.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: If Allah were to grant ample sustenance to His servants they would go about transgressing in the land. But He sends down in due measure whatever (sustenance) He wills. Surely He is Well-Aware and All-Seeing concerning matters that relate to His servants.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And if Allah were to extend the provision for His servants, they would surely rebel in the earth, but He sends down by measure as He wills. Verily, He is, in respect of His servants, the Well-Aware, the All-Seer.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And if Allah were to enlarge the provision for His slaves they would surely rebel in the earth, but He sendeth down by measure as He willeth. Lo! He is Informed, a Seer of His bondmen.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: Were Allah to expand the provision for [all] His servants, they would surely create havoc on the earth. But He sends down in a [precise] measure whatever He wishes. Indeed, He is all-aware, all-seeing about His servants.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: If Allah had expanded His provision to His worshipers, they would become tyrannical in the earth, but He sends down to them what He will in due measure; He is Aware and sees His worshipers.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And if Allah had extended [excessively] provision for His servants, they would have committed tyranny throughout the earth. But He sends [it] down in an amount which He wills. Indeed He is, of His servants, Acquainted and Seeing.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: Had God given abundant sustenance to His servants, they would have certainly rebelled on earth, but He sends them a known measure of sustenance as He wills. He is All-aware of His servants and watches over them all.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And if Allah should amplify the provision for His servants they would certainly revolt in the earth; but He sends it down according to a measure as He pleases; surely He is Aware of, Seeing, His servants.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: If God were to grant His abundant provision to [all] His creatures, they would act insolently on earth, but He sends down in due measure whatever He will, for He is well aware of His servants and watchful over them:

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: If Allah were to enlarge the provision for His Servants, they would indeed transgress beyond all bounds through the earth; but he sends (it) down in due measure as He pleases. For He is with His Servants Well-acquainted, Watchful.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Walaw basata Allahu alrrizqa liAAibadihi labaghaw fee alardi walakin yunazzilu biqadarin ma yashao innahu biAAibadihi khabeerun baseerun

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: It is He who sends down rain when they had despaired of it, and showers His benevolence. He is the protector worthy of praise.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: And it is He Who sends down the rain when they have despaired, and spreads out His mercy; and He is the Benefactor, the Most Praiseworthy.

28.author: Arberry
28.text: And it is He who sends down the rain after they have despaired, and He unfolds His mercy; He is the Protector, the All-laudable.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And He it is Who sendeth down the rain after men have despaired, and spreadeth abroad His mercy. And He is the Patron of all, the Praiseworthy.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: And He it is Who sends down the rain after they have despaired, and spreads abroad His Mercy. And He is the Wali (Helper, Supporter, Protector, etc.), Worthy of all Praise.

28.author: Itani
28.text: It is He who brings down the rain after they have despaired, and unfolds His mercy. He is the Guardian, the Praised.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: He it is Who sends down the rain after they despair of it, spreading out His Mercy. He is the Protector, the Immensely Praiseworthy.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: And He it is Who sends down the rain after they have despaired, and spreads His mercy. And He is the Wali, Worthy of all praise.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: And He it is Who sendeth down the saving rain after they have despaired, and spreadeth out His mercy. He is the Protecting Friend, the Praiseworthy.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: It is He who sends down the rain after they have been despondent, and unfolds His mercy, and He is the Guardian, the All-laudable.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: It is He who sends down rain for them after they despaired, and He unfolds His Mercy. He is the Guardian, the Praised.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And it is He who sends down the rain after they had despaired and spreads His mercy. And He is the Protector, the Praiseworthy.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: It is He who sends down the rain after they had lost hope and spreads out His mercy. He is the Guardian and the Most Praiseworthy.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And He it is Who sends down the rain after they have despaired, and He unfolds His mercy; and He is the Guardian, the Praised One.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: it is He who sends rain after they have despaired and spreads His mercy far and wide. He is the Protector, Worthy of All Praise.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: He is the One that sends down rain (even) after (men) have given up all hope, and scatters His Mercy (far and wide). And He is the Protector, Worthy of all Praise.

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Wahuwa allathee yunazzilu alghaytha min baAAdi ma qanatoo wayanshuru rahmatahu wahuwa alwaliyyu alhameedu

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth and all the living things dispersed in them, are a sign of His. He has the power to gather them together when He will.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the moving creatures dispersed in it; and He is Able to gather them whenever He wills.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and earth and the crawling things He has scattered abroad in them; and He is able to gather them whenever He will.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and of the moving creatures which He hath dispersed in the twain. And He is for their assembling whensoever He will Potent.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: And among His Ayat (proofs, evidences, lessons, signs, etc.) is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and whatever moving (living) creatures He has dispersed in them both. And He is All-Potent over their assembling (i.e. resurrecting them on the Day of Resurrection after their death, and dispersion of their bodies) whenever He will.

29.author: Itani
29.text: And of His signs are the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the creatures He has spread throughout them; and He is Able to gather them at will.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: And of His Signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the living creatures that He has spread out in them. He has the power to bring them together when He so wills.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: And among His Ayat is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and whatever moving creatures He has dispersed in them both. And He is Able to assemble them whenever He wills.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: And of His portents is the creation of the heaven and the earth, and of whatever beasts He hath dispersed therein. And He is Able to gather them when He will.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and whatever creatures He has scattered in them, and He is able to gather them whenever He wishes.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the crawling things which He has dispersed in them. If He will, He is Able to gather all of them.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: And of his signs is the creation of the heavens and earth and what He has dispersed throughout them of creatures. And He, for gathering them when He wills, is competent.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: Some of the evidence (of His existence) are His creation of the heavens and the earth and the beasts which inhabit it. He has all the power to bring them together if He wishes this to be so.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: And one of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and what He has spread forth in both of them of living beings; and when He pleases He is all-powerful to gather them together.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and earth and all the living creatures He has dispersed throughout them: He has the power to gather them all together whenever He will.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: And among His Signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the living creatures that He has scattered through them: and He has power to gather them together when He wills.

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Wamin ayatihi khalqu alssamawati waalardi wama baththa feehima min dabbatin wahuwa AAala jamAAihim itha yashao qadeerun

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: Whatever misfortune befalls you is a consequence of your deeds; yet He forgives much.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: And whatever calamity befalls you, is because of what your hands have earned – and there is a great deal He pardons!

30.author: Arberry
30.text: Whatever affliction may visit you is for what you own hands have earned; and He pardons much.

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: And whatsoever of affliction befalleth you is owing to that which your hands have earned; and He pardoneth much.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: And whatever of misfortune befalls you, it is because of what your hands have earned. And He pardons much. (See the Quran Verse 35:45).

30.author: Itani
30.text: Whatever misfortune befalls you, it is because of what your hands have earned; and yet He pardons much.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: Whatever misfortune befalls you is a consequence of your own deeds. But much of it He forgives.

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: And whatever of misfortune befalls you, it is because of what your hands have earned. And He pardons much.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Whatever of misfortune striketh you, it is what your right hands have earned. And He forgiveth much.

30.author: Qarai
30.text: Whatever affliction that may visit you is because of what your hands have earned, and He excuses many [an offense of yours].

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: If affliction befalls you, it is what your own hands have earned, but He pardons a lot.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And whatever strikes you of disaster - it is for what your hands have earned; but He pardons much.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: Whatever hardship befalls you is the result of your own deeds. God pardons many of your sins.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: And whatever affliction befalls you, it is on account of what your hands have wrought, and (yet) He pardons most (of your faults).

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: Whatever misfortune befalls you is of your own doing, God forgives much,

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Whatever misfortune happens to you, is because on the things your hands have wrought, and for many (of them) He grants forgiveness.

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Wama asabakum min museebatin fabima kasabat aydeekum wayaAAfoo AAan katheerin

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: You cannot thwart Him on the earth, and have no friend or helper apart from Him.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And you cannot escape in the earth; and you have neither a friend nor any supporter against Allah.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: You are not able to frustrate Him in the earth; and, apart from God, you have neither protector nor helper.

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And ye cannot frustrate His Punishment in the earth; and there is for you beside Allah neither protector nor helper.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And you cannot escape from Allah (i.e. His Punishment) in the earth, and besides Allah you have neither any Wali (guardian or a protector) nor any helper.

31.author: Itani
31.text: You are not the ones to interfere on earth; and besides God, you have no ally, and no helper.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: You cannot frustrate Him in the earth; you have no protector nor helper against Allah.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And you cannot escape from Allah in the earth, and besides Allah you have neither any protector nor any helper.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Ye cannot escape in the earth, for beside Allah ye have no protecting friend nor any helper.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: You cannot frustrate [Allah] on the earth, and you do not have besides Allah any guardian or helper.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: You are not able to frustrate Him in the earth, nor do you have a guardian and a helper other than Allah.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And you will not cause failure [to Allah] upon the earth. And you have not besides Allah any protector or helper.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: You cannot challenge God on earth and you will have no one besides Him as your guardian or helper.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And you cannot escape in the earth, and you shall not have a guardian or a helper besides Allah.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: you cannot escape Him anywhere on earth. You have no protector or helper other than God.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: Nor can ye frustrate (aught), (fleeing) through the earth; nor have ye, besides Allah, any one to protect or to help.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Wama antum bimuAAjizeena fee alardi wama lakum min dooni Allahi min waliyyin wala naseerin

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: Ships sailing in the ocean like ensigns are a sign of His.

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: And among His signs are the ships sailing on the sea, like hills.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: And of His signs are the ships that run on the sea like landmarks;

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And of His signs are the ships in the sea like landmarks.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And among His Signs are the ships, in the sea, like mountains.

32.author: Itani
32.text: And of His signs are the ships sailing the sea like flags.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: And of His Signs are the ships that sail in the sea like mountains.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And among His signs are the ships in the sea like mountains.

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And of His portents are the ships, like banners on the sea;

32.author: Qarai
32.text: Among His signs are the ships [that run] on the sea [appearing] like landmarks.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: And among His signs are the ships that run on the sea like mountains and

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And of His signs are the ships in the sea, like mountains.

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: Further evidence (of His existence) are the ships which stand as mountains in the sea.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And among His signs are the ships in the sea like mountains.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: Among His signs are the ships sailing like mountains on the sea:

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: And among His Signs are the ships, smooth-running through the ocean, (tall) as mountains.

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Wamin ayatihi aljawari fee albahri kaalaAAlami

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: He could stop the wind if He pleased, then they would be stranded on its surface. Surely there are signs in this for every one who perseveres and is grateful.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: If He wills, He can calm the winds so the ships remain still on the sea surface; indeed in this are signs for every greatly enduring, grateful person.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: and if He wills, He stills the wind, and they remain motionless on its back. Surely in that are signs for every man enduring, thankful.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: If He willeth He causeth the wind to cease so that they keep still on the back thereof; verily therein are signs for every patient, grateful person.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: If He wills, He causes the wind to cease, then they would become motionless on the back (of the sea). Verily, in this are signs for everyone patient and grateful.

33.author: Itani
33.text: If He willed, He could have stilled the winds, leaving them motionless on its surface. Surely in that are signs for every disciplined, grateful person.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: If He so wills, He can cause the winds to become still so that they will remain motionless on its surface. Surely there are many Signs in this for those who are wont to be steadfast and give thanks.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: If He wills, He causes the wind to cease, then they would become motionless on the surface (of the sea). Verily, in this are signs for everyone patient and grateful.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: If He will He calmeth the wind so that they keep still upon its surface - Lo! herein verily are signs for every steadfast grateful (heart). -

33.author: Qarai
33.text: If He wishes He stills the wind, whereat they remain standstill on its surface. There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful [servant].

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: if He will, He calms the wind so that they remain motionless upon its back, surely, there are signs in this for every thankful, patient (person).

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: If He willed, He could still the wind, and they would remain motionless on its surface. Indeed in that are signs for everyone patient and grateful.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: Had He wanted, He could have stopped the wind and let the ships remain motionless on the surface of the sea, in this there is evidence (of the Truth) for all those who are patient and grateful,

33.author: Shakir
33.text: If He pleases, He causes the wind to become still so that they lie motionless on its back; most surely there are signs in this for every patient, grateful one,

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: if He willed, He could bring the wind to a standstill and they would lie motionless on the surface of the sea, truly there are signs in this for anyone who is steadfast and grateful,

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: If it be His Will He can still the Wind: then would they become motionless on the back of the (ocean). Verily in this are Signs for everyone who patiently perseveres and is grateful.

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: In yasha yuskini alrreeha fayathlalna rawakida AAala thahrihi inna fee thalika laayatin likulli sabbarin shakoorin

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: Or He could wreck them for what they have done; yet there is much that He pardons.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: Or He can ruin them due to peoples’ sins and He can forgive a great deal.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Or He wrecks them for what they have earned; and He pardons much;

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Or He may destroy them because of that which they have earned, and He may pardon many of them.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: Or He may destroy them (by drowning) because of that which their (people) have earned. And He pardons much.

34.author: Itani
34.text: Or He could wreck them, because of what they have earned. And yet He pardons much.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: He may, while forgiving much of the sins of those that ride these ships, drown them on account of some of their misdeeds.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: Or He may destroy them because of that which their (people) have earned. And He pardons much.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Or He causeth them to perish on account of that which they have earned - And He forgiveth much -

34.author: Qarai
34.text: Or He wrecks them because of what they have earned, and He excuses many [an offense].

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Or, He wrecks them for what they have earned, but He pardons a lot.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: Or He could destroy them for what they earned; but He pardons much.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: or He could have destroyed them as punishment for the human being's deeds. However, God pardons many sins.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: Or He may make them founder for what they have earned, and (even then) pardon most;

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: or He may cause them to founder because of people's misdeeds, He pardons many of them,

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: Or He can cause them to perish because of the (evil) which (the men) have earned; but much doth He forgive.

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Aw yoobiqhunna bima kasaboo wayaAAfu AAan katheerin

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Let those who dispute Our signs know that there is no way of escape for them.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: And for those who dispute regarding Our signs to realise; that they do not have a place to escape to.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: and that those who dispute concerning Our signs may know they have no asylum.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: And those who dispute in respect of Our revelations may know that for them there is a place of shelter.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: And those who dispute (polytheists, etc. with Our Messenger Muhammad SAW) as regards Our Ayat (proofs, signs, verses, etc. of Islamic Monotheism) may know that there is no place of refuge for them (from Allah's punishment).

35.author: Itani
35.text: Those who dispute Our signs know that there is no asylum for them.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: Then those who wrangle about Our Signs will come to know that there is no escape for them.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: And those who dispute as regards Our Ayat may know that there is no place of refuge for them.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: And that those who argue concerning Our revelations may know they have no refuge.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Let those who dispute Our signs know that there is no escape for them.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Those who dispute Our verses may know that they have no asylum.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: And [that is so] those who dispute concerning Our signs may know that for them there is no place of escape.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: He knows all those who dispute His revelations. They will find no way to escape from (His torment).

35.author: Shakir
35.text: And (that) those who dispute about Our communications may know; there is no place of refuge for them.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: those who dispute Our signs shall learn that they have no escape.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: But let those know, who dispute about Our Signs, that there is for them no way of escape.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: WayaAAlama allatheena yujadiloona fee ayatina ma lahum min maheesin

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: Whatsoever you have been given is only this life's merchandise; but what is with God is better and more lasting for those who believe and place their trust in their Lord,

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: Whatever you have received is only for usage in the life of this world, and that which is with Allah is much better and more lasting – for those who believe and rely upon their Lord.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: Whatever thing you have been given is the enjoyment of the present life; but what is with God is better and more enduring for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: So whatsoever things are vouchsafed Unto you are but a Passing enjoyment for the life of the world, and that which is with Allah, better and more lasting, is for those who believe and in their Lord put their trust.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: So whatever you have been given is but a passing enjoyment for this worldly life, but that which is with Allah (Paradise) is better and more lasting for those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and put their trust in their Lord (concerning all of their affairs).

36.author: Itani
36.text: Whatever thing you are given is only the provision of this life. But what God possesses is better and more lasting for those who believe and rely on their Lord.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: That which has been given to you is only the wherewithal of the transient life of this world. But that which is with Allah is better and more enduring for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord;

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: So whatever you have been given is but (a passing) enjoyment for this worldly life, but that which is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord.

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: Now whatever ye have been given is but a passing comfort for the life of the world, and that which Allah hath is better and more lasting for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord,

36.author: Qarai
36.text: Whatever you have been given are the wares of the life of this world, but what is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who have faith and who put their trust in their Lord

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: That which you have been given is but the enjoyment of the present life. Better and more enduring is that which Allah has for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: So whatever thing you have been given - it is but [for] enjoyment of the worldly life. But what is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who have believed and upon their Lord rely

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: Whatever you have received is just a means of enjoyment for this life but the reward of God for the believers and those who trust in their Lord will be better and everlasting.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: So whatever thing you are given, that is only a provision of this world's life, and what is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who believe and rely on their Lord.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Whatever you have been given is only a temporary provision of this life, but that which is with God is better and more lasting for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord;

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Whatever ye are given (here) is (but) a convenience of this life: but that which is with Allah is better and more lasting: (it is) for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord:

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Fama ooteetum min shayin famataAAu alhayati alddunya wama AAinda Allahi khayrun waabqa lillatheena amanoo waAAala rabbihim yatawakkaloona

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Who avoid the deadly sins, immoral acts, and forgive when they are angered,

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And (for) those who avoid cardinal sins and indecencies, and forgive (even) when they are angry.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: And those who avoid the heinous sins and indecencies and when they are angry forgive,

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: And those who avoid heinous sins and indecencies, and when they are wroth forgive.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: And those who avoid the greater sins, and Al-Fawahish (illegal sexual intercourse, etc.), and when they are angry, they forgive

37.author: Itani
37.text: And those who avoid major sins and indecencies; and if they become angry, they forgive.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: who eschew grave sins and shameful deeds, and whenever they are angry, forgive;

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: And those who shun the greater sins, and Al-Fawahish, and when they are angry, they forgive.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: And those who shun the worst of sins and indecencies and, when they are wroth, forgive,

37.author: Qarai
37.text: —those who avoid major sins and indecencies, and forgive when angered;

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: And those who avoid the major sins and indecencies and, when angered, forgive;

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: And those who avoid the major sins and immoralities, and when they are angry, they forgive,

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: (This reward will be for) those who keep away from major sins and indecency, who forgive when they are made angry,

37.author: Shakir
37.text: And those who. shun the great sins and indecencies, and whenever they are angry they forgive.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: who refrain from heinous sins and gross indecencies; who forgive when they are angry;

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: Those who avoid the greater crimes and shameful deeds, and, when they are angry even then forgive;

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Waallatheena yajtaniboona kabaira alithmi waalfawahisha waitha ma ghadiboo hum yaghfiroona

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: Who obey the commands of their Lord and fulfil their devotional obligations, whose affairs are settled by mutual consultation, who spend of what We have given them,

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And those who obeyed the command of their Lord and kept the prayer established; and whose affairs are with mutual consultation; and who spend in Our cause from what We have bestowed upon them.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: and those who answer their Lord, and perform the prayer, their affair being counsel between them, and they expend of that We have provided them,

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And those who answer the call of their Lord and establish prayer and whose affair being matter of counsel among themselves, and who of that wherewith We have provided them expend.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And those who answer the Call of their Lord [i.e. to believe that He is the only One Lord (Allah), and to worship none but Him Alone], and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and who (conduct) their affairs by mutual consultation, and who spend of what We have bestowed on them;

38.author: Itani
38.text: And those who respond to their Lord, and pray regularly, and conduct their affairs by mutual consultation, and give of what We have provided them.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: who obey their Lord and establish Prayer; who conduct their affairs by consultation, and spend out of what We have bestowed upon them;

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And those who answer the Call of their Lord, and perform the Salah, and who (conduct) their affairs by mutual consultation, and who spend of what We have bestowed on them.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And those who answer the call of their Lord and establish worship, and whose affairs are a matter of counsel, and who spend of what We have bestowed on them,

38.author: Qarai
38.text: those who answer their Lord, maintain the prayer, and [conduct] their affairs by counsel among themselves, and they spend out of what We have provided them;

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: those who answer their Lord, establish the prayers, and their affairs are by consultation; who spend of that which We have given them,

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And those who have responded to their lord and established prayer and whose affair is [determined by] consultation among themselves, and from what We have provided them, they spend.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: who have pledged their obedience to the Lord, who are steadfast in prayer, who conduct their affairs with consultation among themselves, who spend for the cause of God out of what We have given them,

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And those who respond to their Lord and keep up prayer, and their rule is to take counsel among themselves, and who spend out of what We have given them.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: who respond to their Lord and attend to their prayers; who conduct their affairs by mutual consultation and spend out of what We have provided for them;

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: Those who hearken to their Lord, and establish regular Prayer; who (conduct) their affairs by mutual Consultation; who spend out of what We bestow on them for Sustenance;

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Waallatheena istajaboo lirabbihim waaqamoo alssalata waamruhum shoora baynahum wamimma razaqnahum yunfiqoona

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: And those who defend themselves when they are wronged.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: And those who take revenge when rebellion harms them.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: and who, when insolence visits them, do help themselves --

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: And those who, when they are oppressed vindicate themselves.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: And those who, when an oppressive wrong is done to them, they take revenge.

39.author: Itani
39.text: And those who, when wronged, defend themselves.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: who, when a wrong is done to them, seek its redress.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: And those who, when an oppressive wrong is done to them, take revenge.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: And those who, when great wrong is done to them, defend themselves,

39.author: Qarai
39.text: those who, when afflicted by oppression, defend themselves.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: and when harmed they become victorious.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: And those who, when tyranny strikes them, they defend themselves,

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: and those who, when suffering a great injustice, seek to defend themselves.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: And those who, when great wrong afflicts them, defend themselves.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: who, when they are attacked, defend themselves.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: And those who, when an oppressive wrong is inflicted on them, (are not cowed but) help and defend themselves.

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Waallatheena itha asabahumu albaghyu hum yantasiroona

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: The retribution of evil is the equal of evil (done); yet those who forgive and rehabilitate will be rewarded by God. Verily He does not like those who do wrong.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: The retribution of a harmful deed is the harm equal to it; so whoever forgives and makes amends, so his reward is upon Allah; indeed He does not befriend the unjust.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: and the recompense of evil is evil the like of it; but whoso pardons and puts things right, his wage falls upon God; surely He loves not the evildoers.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: The meed of an ill-deed is an ill the like thereUnto; but whosoever pardoneth and amendeth, his hire is on Allah; verily He approveth not the wrong-doers.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: The recompense for an evil is an evil like thereof, but whoever forgives and makes reconciliation, his reward is due from Allah. Verily, He likes not the Zalimun (oppressors, polytheists, and wrong-doers, etc.).

40.author: Itani
40.text: The repayment of a bad action is one equivalent to it. But whoever pardons and makes reconciliation, his reward lies with God. He does not love the unjust.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: The recompense of evil is evil the like of it. But he who forgives and makes amends, his reward lies with Allah. Surely He does not love the wrong-doers.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: The recompense for an evil is an evil like thereof; but whoever forgives and makes reconciliation, his reward is with Allah. Verily, He likes not the wrongdoers.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: The guerdon of an ill-deed is an ill the like thereof. But whosoever pardoneth and amendeth, his wage is the affair of Allah. Lo! He loveth not wrong-doers.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: The requital of evil is an evil like it, so whoever excuses and conciliates, his reward lies with Allah. Indeed, He does not like the wrongdoers.

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: The recompense of a sin is a sin like it, but whosoever forgives and seeks to reform, his wage will be with Allah. Surely, He does not love the harmdoers.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: And the retribution for an evil act is an evil one like it, but whoever pardons and makes reconciliation - his reward is [due] from Allah. Indeed, He does not like wrongdoers.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: The recompense for evil will be equivalent to the deed. He who pardons (the evil done to him) and reforms himself, will receive his reward from God. God certainly does not love the unjust.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: And the recompense of evil is punishment like it, but whoever forgives and amends, he shall have his reward from Allah; surely He does not love the unjust.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: Let harm be requited by an equal harm. But whoever pardons and amends will find his reward with God. He does not love the wrongdoers.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: The recompense for an injury is an injury equal thereto (in degree): but if a person forgives and makes reconciliation, his reward is due from Allah: for (Allah) loveth not those who do wrong.

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Wajazao sayyiatin sayyiatun mithluha faman AAafa waaslaha faajruhu AAala Allahi innahu la yuhibbu alththalimeena

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: If one avenges himself after he has been wronged, there is no way of blaming him.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And there is no way of reproach against those who take revenge after being wronged.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: And whosoever helps himself after he has been wronged -- against them there is no way.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And whosoever vindicateth him self after wrong done him these! against them there is no way of blame.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: And indeed whosoever takes revenge after he has suffered wrong, for such there is no way (of blame) against them.

41.author: Itani
41.text: As for those who retaliate after being wronged, there is no blame on them.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: There is no blame against him who avenges himself after he has been wronged.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: And indeed whosoever takes revenge after he has suffered wrong, for such there is no way (of blame) against them.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And whoso defendeth himself after he hath suffered wrong - for such, there is no way (of blame) against them.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: As for those who retaliate after being wronged, there is no ground for action against them.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: And whosoever harms after he has been harmed there is no blame upon them.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And whoever avenges himself after having been wronged - those have not upon them any cause [for blame].

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: Those who successfully defend themselves after being wronged will not be questioned.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And whoever defends himself after his being oppressed, these it is against whom there is no way (to blame).

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Those who defend themselves after they have been wronged cannot be held blameworthy,

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: But indeed if any do help and defend themselves after a wrong (done) to them, against such there is no cause of blame.

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Walamani intasara baAAda thulmihi faolaika ma AAalayhim min sabeelin

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: Blame lies on those who oppress, and terrorise the land unjustly. For them there is painful punishment.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Reproach is only against those who oppress people, and wrongfully spread rebellion in the land; for them is a painful punishment.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: The way is only open against those who do wrong to the people, and are insolent in the earth wrongfully; there awaits them a painful chastisement.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: The way of blame is only against those who wrong mankind, and rebel in the earth without justice; these! for them is a torment afflictive.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: The way (of blame) is only against those who oppress men and wrongly rebel in the earth, for such there will be a painful torment.

42.author: Itani
42.text: Blame lies on those who wrong people, and commit aggression in the land without right. These will have a painful punishment.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: Blame attaches only to those who subject people to wrong and commit excesses on earth. A painful chastisement awaits them.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: The way is only against those who oppress men and rebel in the earth without justification; for such there will be a painful torment.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: The way (of blame) is only against those who oppress mankind, and wrongfully rebel in the earth. For such there is a painful doom.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: The ground for action is only against those who oppress the people and commit tyranny in the land in violation of justice. For such there will be a painful punishment.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: The blame is only against those who wrong people, and are wrongfully insolent in the earth, for them there is a painful punishment.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: The cause is only against the ones who wrong the people and tyrannize upon the earth without right. Those will have a painful punishment.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: Only those who do injustice to people and commit rebellion on earth for no reason will be questioned. They will suffer a painful torment.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: The way (to blame) is only against those who oppress men and revolt in the earth unjustly; these shall have a painful punishment.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: blame falls only on those who wrong men and transgress on this earth without justification, such will have a painful punishment,

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: The blame is only against those who oppress men and wrong-doing and insolently transgress beyond bounds through the land, defying right and justice: for such there will be a penalty grievous.

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Innama alssabeelu AAala allatheena yathlimoona alnnasa wayabghoona fee alardi bighayri alhaqqi olaika lahum AAathabun aleemun

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: But he who bears with patience and forgives, surely complies with divine resolve.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: And indeed whoever patiently endured and forgave – then indeed these are acts of great courage.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: But surely he who bears patiently and is forgiving -- surely that is true constancy.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: And whosoever forbeareth and forgiveth -that verily is of the firmness of affairs.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: And verily, whosoever shows patience and forgives that would truly be from the things recommended by Allah.

43.author: Itani
43.text: But whoever endures patiently and forgives—that is a sign of real resolve.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: But he who patiently endures and forgives, that is a conduct of great resolve.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: And verily, whosoever shows patience and forgives, that would truly be from the things recommended by Allah.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: And verily whoso is patient and forgiveth - lo! that, verily, is (of) the steadfast heart of things.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: As for someone who endures patiently and forgives—that is indeed the steadiest of courses.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Surely, he who bears patiently and forgives indeed that is true constancy.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: And whoever is patient and forgives - indeed, that is of the matters [requiring] determination.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: To exercise patience and forgive (the wrong done to one) is the proof of genuine determination.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: And whoever is patient and forgiving, these most surely are actions due to courage.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: whoever is patient and forgiving, acts with great courage and resolution.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: But indeed if any show patience and forgive, that would truly be an exercise of courageous will and resolution in the conduct of affairs.

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Walaman sabara waghafara inna thalika lamin AAazmi alomoori

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: He whom God leads astray has no one except God to protect him. You should see the sinners when they face the punishment. They will say: "Isn't there a way of going back?"

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: And whomever Allah sends astray, there is no friend for him against Allah; and you will see the unjust when they behold the punishment saying, “Is there a way to return?”

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Whomsoever God leads astray, he has no protector after him; and thou shalt see the evildoers, when they see the chastisement, saying, 'Is there any way to be sent back?'

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: And whomsoever Allah will send astray, for him there will be no protecting friend to take His place. And thou wilt behold the wrong- doers when they behold the torment saying: is there Unto return any way?

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: And whomsoever Allah sends astray, for him there is no Wali (protector) after Him. And you will see the Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, oppressors, etc.) when they behold the torment, they will say: "Is there any way of return (to the world)?"

44.author: Itani
44.text: Whoever God leaves astray has no protector apart from Him. And you will see the transgressors, when they see the torment, saying, “Is there a way of going back?”

44.author: Maududi
44.text: He whom Allah lets go astray, none after Him can be his protector. You will see that when the wrong-doers observe the chastisement, they will exclaim: “Is there any way to go back?”

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: And whomsoever Allah sends astray, for him there is no protector after Him. And you will see the wrongdoers, when they behold the torment, they will say: "Is there any way of return"

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: He whom Allah sendeth astray, for him there is no protecting friend after Him. And thou (Muhammad) wilt see the evil-doers when they see the doom, (how) they say: Is there any way of return?

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Those whom Allah leads astray have no guardian apart from Him. You will see the wrongdoers, when they sight the punishment, saying, ‘Is there any way for a retreat?’

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: He whom Allah leads astray has none to protect him thereafter. You will see the harmdoers when they see the punishment saying: 'Is there a way back'

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: And he whom Allah sends astray - for him there is no protector beyond Him. And you will see the wrongdoers, when they see the punishment, saying, "Is there for return [to the former world] any way?"

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: Whomever God has caused to go astray will find no guardian after this. You will see the unjust, on facing the torment, say, "Is there any way to turn back?

44.author: Shakir
44.text: And whomsoever Allah makes err, he has no guardian after Him; and you shall see the unjust, when they see the punishment, saying: Is there any way to return?

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Anyone whom God lets go astray will thereafter have no protector whatsoever: you will see the wrongdoers, when they face the punishment, exclaiming, "Is there no way back?"

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: For any whom Allah leaves astray, there is no protector thereafter. And thou wilt see the Wrong-doers, when in sight of the Penalty, Say: "Is there any way (to effect) a return?"

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Waman yudlili Allahu fama lahu min waliyyin min baAAdihi watara alththalimeena lamma raawoo alAAathaba yaqooloona hal ila maraddin min sabeelin

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: You should see them brought before the Fire, abject in disgrace, looking stealthily. And those who believe will say: "They who forfeit their souls and families on the Day of Resurrection will really be losers." Is it not that sinners will suffer a lasting torment?

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: And you will see them being presented upon the fire, cowering with disgrace watching with concealed eyes; and the believers will say, “Indeed ruined are those who have lost themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection”; pay heed! Indeed the unjust are in a punishment that will never end.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: And thou shalt see them, as they are exposed to it, abject in humbleness, looking with furtive glance; and the believers shall say, 'Surely the losers are they who lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection; surely the evildoers are in lasting chastisement.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: And thou wilt behold them set up before it, downcast with ingominy ond looking with stealthy glance. And those who believe will say: verily the losers are they who have lost themselves and their housefolk on the Day of Resurrection. Lo! verily the wrong-doers will be in a a torment lasting.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: And you will see them brought forward to it (Hell) made humble by disgrace, (and) looking with stealthy glance. And those who believe will say: "Verily, the losers are they who lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Verily, the Zalimun [i.e. Al-Kafirun (disbelievers in Allah, in His Oneness and in His Messenger SAW, polytheists, wrong-doers, etc.)] will be in a lasting torment.

45.author: Itani
45.text: And you will see them exposed to it, cowering from disgrace, looking with concealed eyes. Those who believed will say, “The losers are those who lost themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection.” Indeed, the evildoers are in a lasting torment.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: You shall see them, as they are brought face to face with the chastisement, in a state of abject humiliation, looking with a furtive glance. But the believers will say: “Surely the true losers are they who lose themselves and their kindred on the Day of Resurrection.” Lo, the wrong-doers will be in an enduring torment.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: And you will see them brought forward to it (Hell) made humble by disgrace, (and) looking with stealthy glance. And those who believe will say: "Verily, the losers are they who lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection." Verily, the wrongdoers will be in a lasting torment.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: And thou wilt see them exposed to (the Fire), made humble by disgrace, and looking with veiled eyes. And those who believe will say: Lo! the (eternal) losers are they who lose themselves and their housefolk on the Day of Resurrection. Lo! are not the wrong-doers in perpetual torment?

45.author: Qarai
45.text: You will see them being exposed to it, humbled by abasement, furtively looking askance. The faithful will say, ‘Indeed the losers are those who have ruined themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, the wrongdoers will abide in lasting punishment.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: You shall see them exposed before it, humbled in shame and looking upon it with a furtive glance and the believers will say: 'Indeed, the losers are they who lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection' The harmdoers shall suffer an everlasting punishment.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: And you will see them being exposed to the Fire, humbled from humiliation, looking from [behind] a covert glance. And those who had believed will say, "Indeed, the [true] losers are the ones who lost themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Unquestionably, the wrongdoers are in an enduring punishment."

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: You will see them exposed to the fire, subdued in humiliation, looking sideways at it pleadingly. However, at the same time, the believers will say, "The true losers are those who will lose their souls and families on the Day of Judgment. The unjust will certainly suffer everlasting torment.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: And you shall see them brought before it humbling themselves because of the abasements, looking with a faint glance. And those who believe shall say: Surely the losers are they who have lost themselves and their followers on the resurrection day. Now surely the iniquitous shall remain in lasting chastisement.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: You will see them exposed to the Fire, abject in humiliation, glancing furtively at it, while those who believed will say, "The losers are those ones who have forfeited their souls and their people on the Day of Resurrection." Truly, the wrongdoers will remain in everlasting torment;

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: And thou wilt see them brought forward to the (Penalty), in a humble frame of mind because of (their) disgrace, (and) looking with a stealthy glance. And the Believers will say: "Those are indeed in loss, who have given to perdition their own selves and those belonging to them on the Day of Judgment. Behold! Truly the Wrong-doers are in a lasting Penalty!"

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Watarahum yuAAradoona AAalayha khashiAAeena mina alththulli yanthuroona min tarfin khafiyyin waqala allatheena amanoo inna alkhasireena allatheena khasiroo anfusahum waahleehim yawma alqiyamati ala inna alththalimeena fee AAathabin muqeemin

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: They will have no protector to help them other than God. He whom God allows to go astray has no way.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: And they had no friends to help them against Allah; and there is no way for one whom Allah sends astray.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: They have no protectors to help them, apart from God, and whomsoever God leads astray, no way has he.'

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: And they shall have no patrons succouring them beside Allah. And whomsoever Allah sendeth astray for him there will be no way.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: And they will have no Auliya' (protectors) to help them other than Allah. And he whom Allah sends astray, for him there is no way.

46.author: Itani
46.text: They will have no allies to support them against God. Whomever God leaves astray has no way out.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: They shall have no protectors to help them against Allah. For he whom Allah causes to go astray will have no way to save himself.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: And they will have no protectors to help them other than Allah. And he whom Allah sends astray, for him there is no way.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: And they will have no protecting friends to help them instead of Allah. He whom Allah sendeth astray, for him there is no road.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: They have no awliya to help them besides Allah. There is no way out for those whom Allah leads astray.’

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: They shall have none to protect or help them other than Allah. He whom Allah leads astray there is no way for him.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: And there will not be for them any allies to aid them other than Allah. And whoever Allah sends astray - for him there is no way.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: They will have no guardian or helper besides God. Whoever God has caused to go astray will never find the right direction".

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And they shall have no friends to help them besides Allah; and-- whomsoever Allah makes err, he shall have no way.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: they will have no allies to help them against God; there is no way [forward] for those whom God lets go astray.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: And no protectors have they to help them, other than Allah. And for any whom Allah leaves to stray, there is no way (to the Goal).

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Wama kana lahum min awliyaa yansuroonahum min dooni Allahi waman yudlili Allahu fama lahu min sabeelin

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: Hearken to your Lord before the Day arrives from God that will not be averted. You will have no place of refuge then nor time for denying.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: Obey your Lord before the advent of a Day from Allah, which cannot be averted; you will not have any refuge on that day, nor will you be able to deny.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: Answer your Lord, before there comes a day from God that cannot be turned back; upon that day you shall have no shelter, no denial.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: Answer the call of Your Lord afore there cometh Unto you a Day for which there is no averting from Allah. Ye will have no place of refuge on that Day, nor there will be for you any denying of Your guilt.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: Answer the Call of your Lord (i.e. accept the Islamic Monotheism, O mankind, and jinns) before there comes from Allah a Day which cannot be averted. You will have no refuge on that Day nor there will be for you any denying (of your crimes as they are all recorded in the Book of your deeds).

47.author: Itani
47.text: Respond to your Lord before there comes from God a Day that cannot be turned back. You will have no refuge on that Day, and no possibility of denial.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: Accept the command of your Lord before there comes a Day from Allah that cannot be averted. On that Day there shall be no shelter for you, and none may change your predicament.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: Answer the Call of your Lord before there comes from Allah a Day which cannot be averted. You will have no refuge on that Day nor there will be for you any denying.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: Answer the call of your Lord before there cometh unto you from Allah a Day which there is no averting. Ye have no refuge on that Day, nor have a ye any (power of) refusal.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: Respond to your Lord before there comes a day for which there will be no revoking from Allah. On that day you will have no refuge, nor will you have [any chance for] denial [of your sins].

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: Answer your Lord before that Day arrives which cannot be turned back from Allah, for you on that Day there shall be neither shelter, nor denial.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: Respond to your Lord before a Day comes from Allah of which there is no repelling. No refuge will you have that day, nor for you will there be any denial.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: Pledge obedience to your Lord before the coming of the inevitable Day when you will find no refuge to escape from God's wrath and no one to defend you.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: Hearken to your Lord before there comes the day from Allah for which there shall be no averting; you shall have no refuge on that day, nor shall it be yours to make a denial.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: Respond to your Lord before a Day arrives that will not be averted [against God's will]. You will not find any refuge from God on that Day, nor will you have [any opportunity] to deny your sins.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Hearken ye to your Lord, before there come a Day which there will be no putting back, because of (the Ordainment of) Allah! that Day there will be for you no place of refuge nor will there be for you any room for denial (of your sins)!

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Istajeeboo lirabbikum min qabli an yatiya yawmun la maradda lahu mina Allahi ma lakum min maljain yawmaithin wama lakum min nakeerin

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: If they turn away (you are not responsible); We have not appointed you a warden over them. Your duty is to deliver the message. When We let man taste of Our favours he begins to exult; but if misfortune befalls him, as a consequence of his own deeds, man is surely then ungrateful.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: So if they turn away from you, We have not sent you as a guardian over them; upon you is nothing but to convey (the message); and when We make man taste some mercy from Us, he rejoices upon it; and if some harm reaches them because of what their own hands have sent before, thereupon man is ungrateful!

48.author: Arberry
